input:
## GOALS
- Build strength and muscle
Additional details: Postpartum (6 months), rebuild core strength and pelvic floor function. General fitness, regain pre-pregnancy strength. Gradual progression, no rush.

## TRAINING CONTEXT

### Schedule & Availability
- Desired training frequency: 3

### Experience
- Level: Beginner
- Details: 4 months back to exercise, cleared by doctor. Previously moderately active before pregnancy. Trains Mon/Wed/Fri at 10 AM during baby naps, 30-45 min.

### Equipment & Environment
- Training location: Home gym
- Setup details: Home - resistance bands, 10/15/20 lb dumbbells, exercise ball, yoga mat
- Available equipment: Dumbbells, bands, exercise_ball

### Constraints
- Postpartum diastasis recti, pelvic floor weakness. Working with pelvic PT. Avoids high-impact movements, exercises that cause leaking or coning.

reference.response:
### IDENTITY
- **Name:** Samantha Lee  
- **Age:** 33  
- **Gender:** Female  
- **Experience level:** **Novice** — ~4 months back to exercise postpartum; previously moderately active pre-pregnancy (as of 2026-02-27)

---

### GOALS
- **Primary goal(s):**
  - Build strength and muscle with **gradual progression** (no rush)
  - **Postpartum recovery focus:** rebuild **core strength** and improve **pelvic floor function** (6 months postpartum; as of 2026-02-27)
  - Regain pre-pregnancy strength and general fitness
- **Secondary goals:**
  - Improve overall conditioning while staying **low-impact**
  - Maintain consistency with a sustainable routine (3x/week)

---

### TRAINING CONTEXT

#### Schedule & Availability
- **Available training days:** Monday / Wednesday / Friday
- **Desired training frequency:** 3 sessions/week
- **Session duration:** 30–45 minutes
- **Time window:** 10:00 AM (during baby naps)
- **Blocked days:** Not specified

#### Equipment & Environment
- **Location:** Home gym
- **Available equipment (home):**
  - Dumbbells: **10 / 15 / 20 lb**
  - Resistance bands
  - Exercise ball
  - Yoga mat

#### Constraints
- **[ACTIVE]** Postpartum **diastasis recti** — avoid movements that cause abdominal **coning/doming** (since 2026-02-27). Working with pelvic PT; monitor symptoms each session.
- **[ACTIVE]** **Pelvic floor weakness** — avoid high-impact movements and any exercise that causes **leaking/pressure symptoms** (since 2026-02-27). Coordinate loading/progressions with pelvic PT guidance.

#### Preferences
- **Training style:** Gradual, symptom-guided progression; general fitness/strength emphasis
- **Avoids:** High-impact work; any movements causing leaking or coning
- **Focus areas:** Core + pelvic floor function, full-body strength

---

### METRICS
*(No performance/body composition metrics provided yet; set baseline when available.)*

**Strength (as of 2026-02-27)**
- Baseline lifts/reps: **Not provided yet**

**Movement Quality (postpartum rehab focus; as of 2026-02-27)**
- Symptom constraints tracked: **coning/doming**, **leaking/pressure**
- Pelvic PT involvement: **Yes**
- Pain-free ROM/function milestones: **Not provided yet**

**Body Composition (as of 2026-02-27)**
- Weight / BF%: **Not provided**

---

### LOG
## 2026-02-27 — Profile created/updated (postpartum return to strength)
- Goals added: build strength/muscle; rebuild core + pelvic floor function; gradual progression.
- Training schedule confirmed: **Mon/Wed/Fri**, **10:00 AM**, **30–45 min**, **3x/week**.
- Equipment recorded: home gym with **10/15/20 lb dumbbells**, bands, exercise ball, mat.
- Constraints added: **diastasis recti** and **pelvic floor weakness**; avoiding high-impact and any exercise causing leaking or coning; working with pelvic PT.
- Metrics: baseline values **not yet provided** (to collect for strength + movement quality + body comp).
